steps.title: Identify the key structural marker
steps.content: The passage opens with “Like all species of baleen whales,” establishing a shared characteristic—feeding via baleen plates—before pivoting with “And while...” to contrast the humpback and fin whale.

steps.title: Match to the structure described in the choices
steps.content:
Choice D captures this: it first presents the shared feeding mechanism, then illustrates a difference (size) that is directly relevant to that characteristic, since larger whales consume more krill in absolute terms.
  • A reverses the sequence
  • B invents unmentioned population consequences
  • C wrongly treats baleen feeding as unique to humpbacks
.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
